subtitle: Clear inbox, roster planning today, pay uplift kickoff tomorrow
context: Your inbox is clear with no pending emails as of this morning. You sent two responses yesterday: confirmation to Helena Uddin regarding the occupational health report consent on 8 June, and an update to Julie Hickman on Access Support Team case 68974493 noting that AG requires two items from Oxford University Personnel Services before proceeding. Today at 14:00 you have the GLAM Rostering meeting with Simon Burford on Microsoft Teams to discuss Marie's request regarding roster involvement. Tomorrow morning at 09:00, Jasmin Khokhra is running the Clinical pay uplift 2026 kick off meeting on Microsoft Teams - this is your most time-critical deadline this week on 10 June. Open the Clinical pay uplift meeting details first to prepare for the early 09:00 start tomorrow.

calTomorrow.title: Clinical pay uplift 2026 kick off meeting

priorities.title: Brief team directly
actions: [08 Jun] Identified: team briefing needed — Michael and James already aware, others not yet informed.
[TODO — 09 Jun] Brief remaining team members directly. Bring Wednesday catch-up forward to Tuesday 9 June.
priorities.notes: Kevin returned from leave w/c 2 June on a phased return (4hrs/day, full remote). Team needs to be briefed directly on his return and working arrangements. Michael and James are already across the situation. The others — remaining FA team members — have not yet heard from Kevin directly. Wednesday catch-up was the planned slot but needs bringing forward to Tuesday 9 June.

priorities.title: Smart notes escalation — Cority ticket 00476764
actions: [Nov 2025] Ticket 00476764 raised by James Salas Guillen — option to draft or come back to a note after saving.
[Jan–Apr 2026] Multiple Cority updates from Freeda Franks — dev team still working, no timeline given.
[Apr 2026] Freeda suggested enhanced access support at a cost — position: this is a missing feature, not a chargeable enhancement.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin submitted escalation comment via Cority ticket comment box — requesting committed timeline on CCC-19664, clarification on cost position, and named account manager contact.
[TODO — 16 Jun] Raise at Quality PUG, 2:00 PM (virtual) — escalate formally if no response received from Freeda before then.
[AWAITING] Response from Freeda Franks to escalation comment.
priorities.notes: Smart notes draft-save feature has been missing in Cority for 6+ months. Originally raised by James Salas Guillen. The ability to save a note as a draft or reopen and edit an existing note is not available — users must complete all fields before saving, which is particularly burdensome for longer notes. Cority acknowledged the issue under CCC-19664 and logged it with their dev team. Status is currently 'Pending Future Release' with no committed timeline. Two major releases have passed with no delivery. There is no active account manager, removing the normal escalation route. In April 2026, Freeda Franks (Cority Customer Support Analyst) suggested enhanced access support at a cost — disputed, as this is a missing feature not an enhancement.

priorities.title: Chemistry bulk delete — get details from Julie, pass to Reenu
actions: [Oct 2025] Case 67938206 raised — Chemistry bulk delete of appraisal assignments.
[Apr 2026] Flex points agreed as payment route — case 68974493 raised.
[02 Jun 2026] Marie handed case to Kevin as lead.
[03 Jun 2026] Reenu Delaney requested appraisal cycle name and affected employee list from Kevin — no reply sent.
[08 Jun 2026] Kevin drafted reply to Julie Hickman requesting the required details.
[AWAITING] Julie Hickman to provide appraisal cycle name and affected employee list.
[TODO — on receipt] Forward details straight to Reenu Delaney at AG to action the deletion.
priorities.notes: Chemistry department has incorrectly assigned appraisals in PeopleXD (My Development module). A bulk delete is required. Originally raised as case 67938206 in late 2025 — AG (Reenu Delaney) quoted scripted work via flex points. Flex points case raised in April 2026 as 68974493. Marie Cooksey handed case to Kevin on 2 June 2026. On 3 June, Reenu asked Kevin for the appraisal cycle name and a list of affected employees before she can action the deletion. Ball has been in Oxford's court since 3 June. Julie Hickman is most likely to hold the required details.
